name: Mushroom Pate#3
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Rinse, pat dry, and finely chop mushrooms (makes about 5 cups); set aside.
3. In a large saucepan, melt butter. Add onion and celery; sauté until tender, about 5 minutes; set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat eggs and cream cheese until smooth. Add bread crumbs, salt, basil, rosemary, oregano, black pepper, and the reserved onion and celery mixture and mushrooms. Stir until the mixture is well blended and smooth.
5. Butter a 7 x 4 x 2 inch loaf pan. Cover the long side and bottom of the pan with a strip of waxed paper, leaving a 1-1/2 inch overhang.
6. Spoon the mushroom mixture evenly into the pan.
7. Cover the top of the pan with foil.
8. Bake until firm, about 1-1/2 hours.
9. Cool in the pan until lukewarm.
10. Remove from the pan using the waxed paper overhang as an aid; discard the paper.
11. Serve at room temperature with crackers as an appetizer garnished with mushroom slices, grated carrot, and chopped onion, if desired. Yield: 12 to 14 portions.
